Summary
Incorrect heading hierarchy on the Cookies Policy page prevents information, structure, and relationships from being programmatically determined, impacting screen reader navigation.

image-20260325-162942.png
WCAG Reference

1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A)
Ensures that information and relationships conveyed visually are also available programmatically or in text.

URL
https://fes-ext.pre.private.defra.gov.uk/cookies

Page Title
Cookies Policy – Create a UK catch certificate 

Journey 1, Step 2

Issue Description
Towards the bottom of the page, the heading “Change your cookie settings” is marked up as an <h2>. The question directly beneath it, “Do you want to accept the analytics cookies?”, is also marked up as an <h2>. If these elements are intended to form a hierarchical structure, the heading levels are incorrect.

Current Code Reference



#main-content > div > div > h2:nth-child(35)
<h2 class="govuk-heading-m">Change your cookie settings</h2>
Change

Change the preceding <h2>, to an <h3>. 

Current Level

Changed Level

Text

H2

H2

Change your cookie settings

H2

H3

Do you want to accept the analytics cookies?

Acceptance criteria

The question “Do you want to accept the analytics cookies?” is marked up as an <h3> correctly nested underneath the preceding <h2>.
